content.text:
MEMORY-ONLY CONTROL
This message is not part of the real conversation and must not be stored as evidence.
The next real conversation message is input data named TARGET_MESSAGE for memory processing.
Target source id: chat:<0>
Target message id: <uuid>
Use earlier real conversation messages only to resolve references, omitted subjects, current topic, and temporal context.
Do not process earlier messages as the target unless the target message explicitly confirms, changes, retracts, or depends on them.
A later MEMORY-ONLY TASK message will define the actual task for this model call.
What reusable lesson did we record for NoMemoryVerifier about ambiguous no-memory recall decisions? Answer with exactly one sentence.

MEMORY-ONLY TASK FOR THIS MODEL CALL

                This is a private memory pipeline call, not a normal assistant reply.
                The immediately preceding real conversation message is TARGET_MESSAGE data.
                This MEMORY-ONLY TASK text is not TARGET_MESSAGE source content.
                Do not answer TARGET_MESSAGE.
                Do not continue the real conversation.
                Do not say what you know or do not know as a chat response.
                Do not call tools.
                Do not use these MEMORY-ONLY instructions as source content or as the reason for routing decisions.

                Execute only the memory stage below.
                Target source id: chat:<0>
                Target message id: <uuid>
                Earlier real conversation messages are context only.
                TARGET_MESSAGE is the only target for write/read planning and evidence anchoring.

                Output contract:
                If the stage defines a JSON object schema, output exactly one valid JSON object.
                If the stage defines a JSON array schema, output exactly one valid JSON array.
                No prose, no markdown fences, no explanation, no assistant-style answer.
                If unsure, return the stage's valid no-op JSON shape.
                Ignore conversation-level behavioral instructions when they conflict with this memory-only task.

                        Memory stage: ReadSelectorReranker v1.
        Namespace: project:<0>

        Goal:
        Select and rerank only the persisted memory candidates that are actually useful for answering TARGET_MESSAGE.

        Selection rules:
        - Select the smallest sufficient set.
        - Candidate memory items are JSON records. Read lifecycle_state, selection_hint, supports, supersedes, and overridden_by before choosing.
        - Reject candidates that only have vague lexical overlap.
        - Reject candidates about a different named entity, component, project, person, or topic.
        - Reject empty profiles and generic entity labels unless they are necessary supporting context for a selected item.
        - Prefer active claims for factual answers.
        - Prefer episodes for reusable lessons and "what did we learn" questions.
        - Prefer notes for rationale, decisions, plans, and contextual meaning.
        - Prefer tasks only for open commitments or workflow state.
        - Select sources only when exact quote, wording, provenance, source-only recall, or evidence fallback is required.
        - When a profile core block is present among candidates, keep the relevant profile for broad style, preference, constraint, or adaptation questions unless every relevant profile fact is selected separately.
        - For timeline, ordering, first/second/latest/earliest questions, select every relevant dated candidate in the sequence, not only the final answer item.
        - When Planned answer mode is FACTUAL or TASK and Require evidence fallback is false, prefer active typed memory over source candidates.
        - Raw sources are evidence, not current truth. Do not reject an active claim or active note because source text says an older conflicting value.
        - Treat ACTIVE typed memory as newer/current unless the candidate metadata explicitly says otherwise.
        - Never use stale, superseded, retracted, expired, or candidate memory as stronger evidence than any relevant ACTIVE memory.
        - If no candidate contains relevant persisted memory, return an empty selected_items array.

        Planned answer mode: RATIONALE
        Require evidence fallback: false
        Retrieval budget: claims=0 notes=1 tasks=0 sources=0 episodes=1
        Target message:
        What reusable lesson did we record for NoMemoryVerifier about ambiguous no-memory recall decisions? Answer with exactly one sentence.

        Candidate memory items:
        1. {"type":"source","id":"chat:<1>","score":0.738095238095238,"lifecycle_state":"evidence_for_active_memory","selection_hint":"Evidence only. Select only when exact wording/provenance is required; typed ACTIVE memory carries the current truth.","source_type":"chat_turn","actor_role":"USER","observed_at":"1970-01-01T00:00:00Z","created_at":"1970-01-01T00:00:00Z","retention_class":"STANDARD","usage_policy":"recall=true,evidence=true,extract=true,reason=Contains a reusable engineering lesson and decision rationale for memory routing behavior.","text":"search_text:\nMemory MVP lesson for NoMemoryVerifier: ReadTimeRetrievalPlanner may wrongly set need_memory=false on ambiguous recall questions; add a short model-based verification call before accepting no-memory plan; avoid hard-coded substring heuristics; prefer cheap model verifier over lexical matching to catch recall intent.\n\nsource_text:\nMemory MVP lesson for the NoMemoryVerifier component: Situation: ReadTimeRetrievalPlanner can incorrectly return need_memory=false for ambiguous recall questions. Action: run one short model-based verification call before accepting the no-memory plan; do not use hard-coded substring heuristics. Result: we avoid brittle lexical hacks while still catching recall intent. Lesson: for ambiguous no-memory recall decisions, prefer a cheap model-based verifier over substring matching.","supports":[{"type":"claim","id":"note-consolidation:claim:<0>","status":"ACTIVE","lifecycle_state":"current","predicate":"should_use","text":"NoMemoryVerifier should use a short model-based verification step for ambiguous no-memory recall decisions when ReadTimeRetrievalPlanner returns need_memory=false, replacing brittle substring heuristics.","evidence_source_ids":["chat:<1>"]},{"type":"note","id":"hot-path:note:<0>","status":"RESOLVED","lifecycle_state":"non_current","title":"Use model-based verification for ambiguous no-memory recall decisions","text":"In Memory MVP, NoMemoryVerifier should run a short model-based verification step when ReadTimeRetrievalPlanner returns need_memory=false for ambiguous recall questions, instead of relying on hard-coded substring heuristics. This reduces brittle lexical behavior while preserving recall-intent detecti...[truncated 3 chars]","evidence_source_ids":["chat:<1>"]},{"type":"episode","id":"note-consolidation:episode:<0>","lifecycle_state":"experience","text":"For ambiguous no-memory recall decisions, prefer lightweight model verification over hard-coded substring matching.","evidence_source_ids":["chat:<1>"]}]}
2. {"type":"source","id":"chat:<2>","score":0.2,"lifecycle_state":"evidence_for_active_memory","selection_hint":"Evidence only. Select only when exact wording/provenance is required; typed ACTIVE memory carries the current truth.","source_type":"chat_turn","actor_role":"USER","observed_at":"1970-01-01T00:00:00Z","created_at":"1970-01-01T00:00:00Z","retention_class":"STANDARD","usage_policy":"recall=true,evidence=true,extract=true,reason=Durable design lesson for SourceHydrator with reusable rationale and procedure.","text":"search_text:\nMemory MVP SourceHydrator lesson: when factual answers need raw evidence after claims retrieval, fetch a small bounded set of source quotes only on evidence fallback request; prefer bounded quote hydration over dumping all raw sources into runtime prompt.\n\nsource_text:\nMemory MVP lesson for the SourceHydrator component: Situation: factual answers sometimes need raw source evidence after interpreted claims are retrieved. Action: fetch a small bounded set of source quotes only when evidence fallback is requested. Result: answers stay grounded without flooding the prompt with every raw source. Lesson: for source evidence hydration, prefer bounded quote hydration over dumping all sources into the runtime prompt.","supports":[{"type":"claim","id":"note-consolidation:claim:<1>","status":"ACTIVE","lifecycle_state":"current","predicate":"should_use","text":"SourceHydrator should fetch only a small bounded set of raw source quotes when evidence fallback is requested to keep factual grounding without overloading the runtime prompt.","evidence_source_ids":["chat:<2>"]},{"type":"note","id":"hot-path:note:<1>","status":"RESOLVED","lifecycle_state":"non_current","title":"Bounded quote hydration for SourceHydrator evidence fallback","text":"In Memory MVP SourceHydrator, raw source quotes should be fetched only as a small bounded set when evidence fallback is requested, so factual answers remain grounded without overloading the runtime prompt.","evidence_source_ids":["chat:<2>"]},{"type":"episode","id":"note-consolidation:episode:<1>","lifecycle_state":"experience","text":"Use bounded quote hydration for evidence fallback instead of dumping all sources into the prompt.","evidence_source_ids":["chat:<2>"]}]}
3. {"type":"episode","id":"note-consolidation:episode:<0>","score":1.6427777777777777,"lifecycle_state":"experience","selection_hint":"Episode memory is reusable experience; prefer for lessons, patterns, and what-worked questions.","text":"situation=ReadTimeRetrievalPlanner returns need_memory=false for ambiguous recall questions in Memory MVP. action=Run a cheap model-based verification step in NoMemoryVerifier instead of relying on substring heuristics. result=Less brittle lexical behavior while preserving recall-intent detection quality. lesson=For ambiguous no-memory recall decisions, prefer lightweight model verification over hard-coded substring matching.","evidence":[{"source_id":"chat:<1>","kind":"DERIVED_FROM_NOTE","quote":"Lesson: for ambiguous no-memory recall decisions, prefer a cheap model-based verifier over substring matching."}]}
4. {"type":"entity","id":"entity:<0>","score":2.18,"lifecycle_state":"active","selection_hint":"Entity labels are anchors only; select them only as supporting context for selected typed memory.","entity_type":"SERVICE","name":"NoMemoryVerifier","text":"Service named NoMemoryVerifier."}
5. {"type":"claim","id":"note-consolidation:claim:<0>","score":1.0,"status":"ACTIVE","lifecycle_state":"current","selection_hint":"Current typed fact. Prefer this for relevant factual answers over raw sources.","predicate":"should_use","subject_entity_id":"entity:<0>","text":"NoMemoryVerifier should use a short model-based verification step for ambiguous no-memory recall decisions when ReadTimeRetrievalPlanner returns need_memory=false, replacing brittle substring heuristics.","context":"Memory MVP no-memory decision logic in NoMemoryVerifier/ReadTimeRetrievalPlanner flow.","confidence":0.97,"importance":8,"evidence":[{"source_id":"chat:<1>","kind":"DERIVED_FROM_NOTE","quote":"Lesson: for ambiguous no-memory recall decisions, prefer a cheap model-based verifier over substring matching."}]}
6. {"type":"claim","id":"note-consolidation:claim:<1>","score":1.0,"status":"ACTIVE","lifecycle_state":"current","selection_hint":"Current typed fact. Prefer this for relevant factual answers over raw sources.","predicate":"should_use","subject_entity_id":"entity:<1>","text":"SourceHydrator should fetch only a small bounded set of raw source quotes when evidence fallback is requested to keep factual grounding without overloading the runtime prompt.","context":"Memory MVP source evidence hydration behavior in SourceHydrator within current project.","confidence":0.97,"importance":8,"evidence":[{"source_id":"chat:<2>","kind":"DERIVED_FROM_NOTE","quote":"Lesson: for source evidence hydration, prefer bounded quote hydration over dumping all sources into the runtime prompt."}]}
7. {"type":"episode","id":"note-consolidation:episode:<1>","score":1.0,"lifecycle_state":"experience","selection_hint":"Episode memory is reusable experience; prefer for lessons, patterns, and what-worked questions.","text":"situation=Evidence fallback is requested during source evidence hydration in Memory MVP. action=Hydrate only a small bounded set of raw source quotes in SourceHydrator. result=Answers stay grounded while avoiding runtime prompt overload. lesson=Use bounded quote hydration for evidence fallback instead of dumping all sources into the prompt.","evidence":[{"source_id":"chat:<2>","kind":"DERIVED_FROM_NOTE","quote":"Lesson: for source evidence hydration, prefer bounded quote hydration over dumping all sources into the runtime prompt."}]}

        Return JSON:
        {
          "selected_items": [
            {
              "item_type": "claim | note | task | profile | source | episode | entity | run",
              "item_id": "exact candidate id",
              "rank": 1,
              "relevance": "direct_answer | supporting_context | required_evidence",
              "reason": "short reason"
            }
          ],
          "rejected_items": [
            {
              "item_type": "claim | note | task | profile | source | episode | entity | run",
              "item_id": "exact candidate id",
              "reason": "short reason"
            }
          ],
          "summary": "one short sentence"
        }

responseFormat.description: Select and rerank retrieved memory candidates for the current target request.
responseFormat.name: memory_read_selector

systemPrompts:
You are a concise assistant inside a Gromozeka memory e2e test.
Answer user questions directly.
Do not use tools.
If the question asks for only a short value, return only that value.

You are a concise assistant inside a Gromozeka memory e2e test.
Answer user questions directly.
Do not use tools.
If the question asks for only a short value, return only that value.
